phase: Phase 7 Display / Graphics Minimal Full Vertical
scope: Add DisplayCleanupRecord as first-class semantic display cleanup evidence
Add CleanupDisplay command and DisplayCleanupStarted/Completed events
Release active handle-mode FramebufferMapping objects as cleanup effects
Release active FramebufferWindowLease objects as cleanup effects
Revoke DisplayCapability and its underlying store-local CapabilityHandle generation-safely
Preserve historical framebuffer write/flush/dirty/event-log evidence after cleanup
Expose display-cleanup stable ViewV1 and graph cleanup-effect edges through osctl
no_goals: real framebuffer present or scanout flush
snapshot barrier enforcement for active framebuffer leases

findings_fixed: Display cleanup uses exact object refs and generations instead of display/debug labels
FramebufferMapping and FramebufferWindowLease cleanup effects are not modeled as live ownership
DisplayCapability revocation advances the underlying capability generation while preserving the display-capability object generation
Historical framebuffer write and display event-log evidence remains valid after cleanup
New framebuffer writes through an unmapped handle are rejected
bad_smells_checked: No real display backend or present path was added
No raw framebuffer pointer became semantic truth
No third-party graphics dependency was introduced
osctl emits ViewV1 JSON rather than internal Rust structs
cleanup effects are visible through ContractGraph cleanup-effect edges
residual_notes: G10 owns snapshot barrier enforcement for active framebuffer leases, mappings, and dirty regions

residual_gaps: G10 owns snapshot barrier enforcement for active framebuffer leases, mappings, and dirty regions
